title: PacBio Announces Second Quarter 2024 Financial Results
text: PacBio reported total revenue of $36.0 million for Q2 2024, down from $47.6 million year-over-year. Instrument revenue declined to $14.7 million from $29.9 million, with 24 Revio sequencing systems delivered during the quarter. Consumables revenue was $17.0 million compared with $13.7 million in the prior-year period. Service and other revenue was $4.3 million compared with $3.9 million in the prior-year period. Net loss for Q2 2024 was $173.3 million, compared to a net loss of $69.8 million for Q2 2023. Net loss per share was $0.64 compared to $0.28 for Q2 2023. On an adjusted basis, PacBio delivered an adjusted loss per share of 20 cents, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 16.7%. CEO Christian Henry noted the company continued to see headwinds in the capital equipment market, particularly in Europe and Asia. The company expressed optimism about future growth, stating it expects revenue will accelerate in the second half of 2024 and into 2025. The quarter included a significant $93.2 million goodwill impairment charge related to stock price decline. Management indicated it remains on track to reduce annual run rate expenses by more than $75 million without compromising key product development initiatives. Full-year revenue is now expected to be around the low end of the previously guided range of $170 million to $200 million.
